PPT Outline Generation Strategy
Experimental Report
Analysis of Prompt Strategies, Schema Constraints, and Model Performance in LLM-Generated
Outlines

trategies, Schema Constraints, and Model Performance in LLM-Generated
Outlines

1. Experiment Overview
This report presents a systematic evaluation of how different prompt strategies and structured
output constraints affect the quality, speed, and coherence of PPT outline generation by large
language models (LLMs). The study covers three dimensions:
• Prompt Strategy: Baseline, CoT Silent (Chain of Thought), and Few-Shot prompting.
• Schema Constraints: Schema Off (free-form generation) vs. Schema On (forced JSON
format).

straints: Schema Off (free-form generation) vs. Schema On (forced JSON
format).
• Model Comparison: DeepSeek, Qwen (Tongyi Qianwen), and GLM (Zhipu Qingyan).

• Model Comparison: DeepSeek, Qwen (Tongyi Qianwen), and GLM (Zhipu Qingyan).

All experiments used the DeepSeek model for the prompt/schema study, and all three models
for the cross-model comparison. The task across all tests was generating a structured PPT
outline for "A Brief History of Artificial Intelligence Development." The experimental design is
orthogonal — each variable is analyzed independently and in combination to isolate its
individual and compound effects.

dependently and in combination to isolate its
individual and compound effects.

Key observations at a glance: Few-Shot consistently achieves the shortest generation time; CoT
Silent + Schema On produces the highest information density (4.62 bullet points per page); and
enabling Schema reduces generation time by approximately 15–25% across all strategies.

Schema reduces generation time by approximately 15–25% across all strategies.

3. Analysis of Prompt Strategies
With Schema conditions held constant, the three prompt strategies exhibit meaningfully different
cognitive and generative behaviors.

strategies exhibit meaningfully different
cognitive and generative behaviors.

3.1 Few-Shot Prompting — Efficiency and Specification
Few-Shot consistently delivers the shortest generation time across both schema conditions
(49.6s with Schema Off; 43.4s with Schema On). Providing concrete examples dramatically
reduces the model's trial-and-error overhead in formatting and content framing, resulting in
output that is highly aligned with the expected structure. The trade-off is reduced creative

s highly aligned with the expected structure. The trade-off is reduced creative
latitude — the output closely mirrors the provided examples in tone and organization.

e — the output closely mirrors the provided examples in tone and organization.

3.2 CoT Silent — Logic and Depth
Chain of Thought prompting enables the model to break down complex historical narratives into
structured, causally-linked segments. This approach shines when generating content that
requires sequential reasoning — for example, tracing how the "knowledge engineering
bottleneck" of expert systems led inevitably to the rise of statistical learning. Generation time
stays in the middle range, and this strategy produces the most logically coherent chapter

he middle range, and this strategy produces the most logically coherent chapter
progressions.

, and this strategy produces the most logically coherent chapter
progressions.

3.3 Baseline — Details and Breadth
Without any guidance, the model draws heavily on its internal knowledge base, often producing
rich background details and clear audience targeting. However, this exhaustive retrieval comes
at a high cost: generation time reaches nearly 74 seconds — the highest across all conditions.
The Baseline strategy is most vulnerable to structural collapse under schema constraints (see
Section 5.3).

vulnerable to structural collapse under schema constraints (see
Section 5.3).

PPT Outline Generation Strategy — Experimental Report  |  Page 3
4. Analysis of Schema Constraints
Comparing Schema Off versus Schema On across all prompt strategies reveals three
consistent, independent effects.

On across all prompt strategies reveals three
consistent, independent effects.

4.1 Computational Acceleration
Enabling Schema reduces generation time by 15–25% across all prompt strategies. The strict
attribute-value structure of the JSON format narrows the model's output search space,
eliminating the overhead of exploratory free-form formatting. This makes Schema On the single
most effective lever for reducing generation latency in a production context.

most effective lever for reducing generation latency in a production context.

4.2 Increased Information Density
Under Schema Off, the average number of bullet points per page ranges from 3.20 to 3.93.
Under Schema On, this rises uniformly above 4.00 (peaking at 4.62). The JSON array structure
acts as an implicit pressure — the model fills each structured slot more completely to satisfy the
format's integrity requirements, producing denser slides.

etely to satisfy the
format's integrity requirements, producing denser slides.

4.3 Breaking the "Default Safety Template"
In the unconstrained condition, all three prompt strategies converge on 6 chapters — a
reflection of the model's default structural instinct. When Schema constraints are applied, this
default is disrupted: chapter counts range from 4 to 7, revealing the model's true planning
divergence when forced to commit to a rigid schema. This is a double-edged effect: it unlocks

n forced to commit to a rigid schema. This is a double-edged effect: it unlocks
richer structural variety but also exposes vulnerabilities (see Baseline + Schema On in Section
5.3).

ty but also exposes vulnerabilities (see Baseline + Schema On in Section
5.3).

5. Cross-Variable Interaction Effects
The combination of prompt strategy and schema constraint does not produce simple additive
effects. Instead, the interactions reveal distinct compound behaviors.

dditive
effects. Instead, the interactions reveal distinct compound behaviors.

5.1 Few-Shot + Schema On — Most Deterministic
Few-Shot provides a content template; Schema On provides a format lock. Together, they place
the model in a highly efficient "assembly line" mode, producing a well-structured outline in just
43.4 seconds with stable quality. This combination is ideal for production environments with
strict requirements on both response speed and output consistency.

nments with
strict requirements on both response speed and output consistency.

PPT Outline Generation Strategy — Experimental Report  |  Page 4
5.2 CoT Silent + Schema On — Most In-Depth
When Schema On absorbs the cognitive burden of formatting decisions, Chain of Thought
reasoning is freed entirely for content decomposition. The result: 7 chapters (the most across all
conditions) and the highest micro-information density at 4.62 bullet points per page. This pairing
is best suited for academic, analytical, or research-grade deliverables that prioritize logical rigor

demic, analytical, or research-grade deliverables that prioritize logical rigor
and granular detail over generation speed.

ables that prioritize logical rigor
and granular detail over generation speed.

5.3 Baseline + Schema On — Most Unstable
Without either the concrete template of Few-Shot or the reasoning scaffolding of CoT, the
Baseline model struggles under schema constraints. Facing strict JSON requirements with no
structural guidance, it "ends macro planning early" — collapsing from the standard 6 chapters
down to just 4. It compensates by overloading the remaining micro-nodes with content (4.09

t 4. It compensates by overloading the remaining micro-nodes with content (4.09
bullet points per page). This pattern — fewer chapters, denser individual slides — is a symptom
of planning imbalance: the model sees the trees but loses the forest. The Baseline + Schema
On combination should be avoided in constrained production environments.

chema
On combination should be avoided in constrained production environments.

6. Cross-Model Comparison (Schema Off)
A parallel study compared DeepSeek, Qwen, and GLM on the same PPT outline task to assess
differences in engineering reliability, structural design, logical coherence, and pagination
granularity.

reliability, structural design, logical coherence, and pagination
granularity.

6.1 Engineering Reliability and Format Compliance
• DeepSeek & Qwen both generated complete, well-formed JSON that conformed to the
expected hierarchical logic and could be used directly by downstream parsing programs.
• GLM failed to produce valid JSON due to a syntax error — double quotes inside string
values were not properly escaped (e.g. proposed the \"Turing test\"), causing a parse
exception. This highlights GLM's weakness in structured output robustness when

exception. This highlights GLM's weakness in structured output robustness when
schema constraints are absent.

s weakness in structured output robustness when
schema constraints are absent.

6.3 Hierarchical Structure and Chapter Logic
All three models follow the broad arc of "origins → stagnation → breakthrough → challenges →
future", but their emphasis and chapter design differ significantly:
• DeepSeek frames the timeline as a series of narrative "Acts", with particularly detailed
coverage of post-2010 developments: the architecture (Transformer), the phenomenon
(ChatGPT), and the competitive landscape (China vs. global).

r), the phenomenon
(ChatGPT), and the competitive landscape (China vs. global).
• Qwen organizes the narrative around paradigm shifts — from rule-driven to data-driven
— and closes with engineering-relevant takeaways, making it structurally suited for
course syllabi.
• GLM uses a more traditional 4-chapter taxonomy. While it covers key milestones, the
transitions between chapters lack sufficient causal explanation, resulting in a more fact-
list feel.

pters lack sufficient causal explanation, resulting in a more fact-
list feel.

6.4 Logical Coherence Analysis
• Qwen shows the strongest logical consistency. Its outline doesn't just chronicle events
— it explains why each phase emerged. The "Challenges and Future" chapter traces a
coherent argument from technical flaws (hallucinations) to social cost (energy
consumption) to fundamental values (alignment).
• DeepSeek traces technological evolution clearly by pairing each era's bottleneck with its

races technological evolution clearly by pairing each era's bottleneck with its
corresponding breakthrough, making the inevitability of each transition explicit.
• GLM's logic is comparatively fragmented — strong on facts, weaker on the connective
tissue between chapters.

ragmented — strong on facts, weaker on the connective
tissue between chapters.

7. Model Behavior Under Schema Constraints
Enabling Schema constraints changes each model's behavior in distinct ways, beyond simply
enforcing format compliance.

7.1 Engineering Reliability: A Decisive Improvement

orcing format compliance.

7.1 Engineering Reliability: A Decisive Improvement

PPT Outline Generation Strategy — Experimental Report  |  Page 6
The most dramatic change is the resolution of GLM's JSON validity problem. In Schema Off
mode, GLM's output failed to parse entirely. In Schema On mode, it successfully generated fully
compliant JSON, with metadata validation confirming schema_ok: true. Across all three models,
Schema On ensures that structured metadata — page counts, chapter counts, quality metrics
— is consistently accessible to downstream programs.

r counts, quality metrics
— is consistently accessible to downstream programs.

7.2 Content Granularity Shifts
Enabling constraints pushes each model's content density in a different direction:

ling constraints pushes each model's content density in a different direction:

Model Schema Off Schema On Change Trend

focused; merges fragmented
sub-pages; tighter structure.
Qwen 11 slides / 6 chapters 16 slides / 7 chapters Elaborated: Significantly
expanded technical details,
adding standalone pages for
NLP transition and global
competitive landscape.
GLM Parse failed (~11
pages raw text)

chaotic raw text to a well-

chaotic raw text to a well-
structured 5-chapter outline.

These divergent responses reflect each model's underlying planning strategy: DeepSeek
consolidates; Qwen expands; GLM standardizes. None of these responses are inherently wrong
— they represent different strengths made more legible by the constraint.

rong
— they represent different strengths made more legible by the constraint.

7.3 Structural Rigor and Logical Depth
Schema On also raises the analytical quality of content across the board:
• DeepSeek adds a dedicated page on the "AI Capability Evolution Path" (perceptual
intelligence → cognitive → generative → decision-making) and explicitly addresses
explainable AI (XAI) and alignment in its future section — creating a more closed-loop
logical arc.
• Qwen fills in previously missing coverage of the 1990s–2000s (SVM, Random Forest,

en fills in previously missing coverage of the 1990s–2000s (SVM, Random Forest,
Bayesian Networks) and adds quantitative comparison data to speaker notes (e.g.
MMLU scores 86.4 vs. 83.2), elevating the outline from a title list to a reference
document.
• GLM establishes a clear 5-stage narrative framework (Origin → Renaissance →
Revolution → Era → Future) and adds concrete quantitative anchors to the Challenges
chapter, such as the carbon emissions equivalence of a single training run.

es
chapter, such as the carbon emissions equivalence of a single training run.

In summary, Schema On functions as more than a format check — it acts as implicit prompt
steering, compelling more deliberate layout decisions, resolving JSON instability in long-form

PPT Outline Generation Strategy — Experimental Report  |  Page 7
generation, and nudging all models toward more technical and precisely classified language.

8. Conclusions and Recommendations

chnical and precisely classified language.

8. Conclusions and Recommendations

8.1 Key Findings
1. Constraints accelerate generation. Providing a clear output Schema is the single most
effective way to reduce latency, while simultaneously improving downstream parse
reliability.
2. Strategy determines structural quality. Schema alone is insufficient — the right prompt
strategy must accompany it. Few-Shot for speed and stability; CoT for depth and
information density; avoid Baseline in constrained environments to prevent
macrostructure collapse.

avoid Baseline in constrained environments to prevent
macrostructure collapse.
3. Generation speed, logical depth, and content density form an impossible triangle. The
optimal combination depends on task requirements and should be chosen deliberately.

al combination depends on task requirements and should be chosen deliberately.

8.2 Model Rankings and Use Cases
Based on comprehensive quality across engineering reliability, logical coherence, and
pagination granularity:

across engineering reliability, logical coherence, and
pagination granularity:

• DeepSeek (1st): Best for high-reliability, high-granularity production PPT outlines,
particularly when covering post-2010 technical developments. The most scientifically
structured pagination of the three.
• Qwen (2nd): Best for teaching, course design, or engineering reference contexts.
Superior logical rigor and quantified data support make it ideal as a software engineering
course syllabus.
• GLM (3rd): Underperforms without schema constraints due to format parsing errors.

M (3rd): Underperforms without schema constraints due to format parsing errors.
Becomes usable with Schema On, but remains thinner in content depth compared to the
other two.

ith Schema On, but remains thinner in content depth compared to the
other two.

8.3 Recommended Configurations
Based on the full experimental analysis, the following configurations are recommended for
specific use cases:

• Production environment (speed + format stability required): Few-Shot + Schema On
using DeepSeek.
• Academic or analytical deliverable (depth + density required): CoT Silent + Schema On
using DeepSeek or Qwen.
• Teaching or course material: Qwen with Schema On, any prompt strategy.

Qwen.
• Teaching or course material: Qwen with Schema On, any prompt strategy.

PPT Outline Generation Strategy — Experimental Report  |  Page 8
• Avoid in all cases: Baseline + Schema On with any model (risk of macrostructure
collapse).

End of Report
